column1: DOP-C02,"Domain 3: Resilient Architectures","Resilient Cloud Solutions","## DOP-C02 Domain 3: Resilient Cloud Solutions
This domain covers highly available architectures, disaster recovery strategies, and automated self-healing. Everything must be automated - manual recovery is a wrong answer.

column3: Scale by different amounts based on severity

column1: **Health Check Grace Period:** Time after launch before checks begin. Set based on app startup time. Too short = launch loop.
Scale-Out Hook: Install software, pull config, validate, warm caches
Scale-In Hook: Drain connections, deregister, push logs, complete requests

column3: Rolling replacement of all instances (new launch template)
Pause at specific percentages for validation
Don't replace instances already matching config

column1: WITHOUT WARM POOL: Scale-out -> Launch -> Boot (2 min) -> Configure (5 min) = ~7 min
WITH WARM POOL (Stopped): Scale-out -> Start pre-initialized instance = ~1-2 min
WITH WARM POOL (Running): Scale-out -> Attach to target group = ~30 sec
- ELB stops NEW requests; existing requests complete
- Set higher for long-running connections (uploads, WebSocket)
- Primary cluster (writer) + secondary clusters (readers) in other regions
- Managed planned failover: < 1 minute (no data loss)
- Write forwarding: secondary can forward writes to primary

column3: Prevent routing to zero healthy regions

column1: - Automatically rolls back failed ECS deployments
- If new tasks keep failing to reach RUNNING state -> rollback

column3: EC2, EBS, RDS, Aurora, DynamoDB, EFS, FSx, S3
CPU stress, network latency, instance stop, AZ failure

column1: - **""Custom metric scaling""** -> Target Tracking with custom CloudWatch metric
- **""Instances take 10 min to configure""** -> Warm Pools
- **""Drain connections before terminating""** -> Lifecycle Hook + deregistration delay
- **""Replace all instances with new AMI""** -> Instance Refresh
- **""Instances failing health checks immediately""** -> Increase Health Check Grace Period
- **""RPO < 1 sec, RTO < 1 min for database""** -> Aurora Global Database
- **""Active-active database across regions""** -> DynamoDB Global Tables
- **""Instant failover without DNS TTL""** -> Global Accelerator
- **""Same instance, same IP after hardware failure""** -> EC2 Auto Recovery
- **""ECS deployment keeps failing""** -> Deployment Circuit Breaker
- **""Workflow step fails""** -> Step Functions Retry with BackoffRate
- **Health Check Grace Period** prevents launch loops. Set >= app startup time.
- **Warm Pools** solve slow scaling. Stopped = EBS cost only. Running = instant.
- **Instance Refresh** rolls out new AMIs. Checkpoints pause for validation. Auto-rollback on alarm.
- **Mixed Instances Policy:** OnDemandBaseCapacity ensures reliable baseline.
- **Lifecycle Hooks** pause launch/terminate for custom actions. Always signal CONTINUE when done.
- **Aurora Global Database** = cross-region DB DR with < 1 sec RPO, < 1 min RTO.
- **DynamoDB Global Tables** = active-active (multi-region writes). No failover needed.
- **Route 53 ARC** = safe failover with readiness checks and safety rules.
- **Global Accelerator** = instant failover (no DNS TTL). Static IPs. TCP/UDP.
- **ECS Circuit Breaker** auto-rolls back failed deployments. No manual intervention.
- **Step Functions Retry** with BackoffRate = resilient workflows.
- **FIS** = chaos engineering. Always define stop conditions (CloudWatch alarms).
- **S3 Replication Time Control** guarantees 99.99% replicated within 15 min.
- **AWS Backup Vault Lock** = WORM. No one can delete before retention expires.
- **Deregistration Delay** = time for in-flight requests. 0 for stateless; higher for long connections.
- **EC2 Auto Recovery** = same instance/IP on new hardware. ASG = new instance.
This covers all objectives in DOP-C02 Domain 3:
- **Auto Scaling** - Policies, health checks, lifecycle hooks, instance refresh, warm pools, mixed instances
- **Load Balancing** - ALB/NLB, weighted target groups, deregistration delay, slow start
- **Disaster Recovery** - Backup/Restore, Pilot Light, Warm Standby, Active-Active
- **Self-Healing** - ASG, EC2 Auto Recovery, ECS Circuit Breaker, EventBridge+Lambda
- **Multi-Region** - Aurora Global, DynamoDB GT, S3 CRR, AWS Backup
- **Chaos Engineering** - AWS FIS, experiments, stop conditions

column2: +-- Scaling Policies: Target Tracking, Step, Scheduled, Predictive
+-- Health Checks: EC2, ELB, Custom; Grace Period
+-- Instance Refresh: Rolling AMI updates; checkpoints; auto-rollback
+-- Mixed Instances: On-Demand base + Spot; multiple instance types
+-- Cooldown: Prevent rapid scale in/out oscillation
+-- ALB: Layer 7; path/host routing; weighted target groups
+-- NLB: Layer 4; static IP; ultra-low latency
+-- Features: Slow start, deregistration delay, cross-zone, sticky
+-- Health Checks: HTTP/TCP; interval, threshold, grace period
+-- RDS Multi-AZ: Synchronous standby; automatic failover (1-2 min)
+-- Aurora: 6 copies/3 AZs; < 30 sec failover; up to 15 replicas
+-- Managed services: S3, DynamoDB, Lambda (automatic multi-AZ)
+-- Strategies: Backup/Restore, Pilot Light, Warm Standby, Active-Active
+-- Data Replication: S3 CRR, Aurora Global, DynamoDB GT, EFS Replication
+-- AWS Backup: Plans, vaults, cross-region, cross-account, vault lock
+-- Testing: Regular DR drills; FIS; GameDays
+-- Route 53: Failover, Latency, Weighted, Geolocation routing
+-- Health Checks: Endpoint, Calculated, CloudWatch Alarm
+-- Route 53 ARC: Readiness checks, routing controls, safety rules
+-- Global Accelerator: Static IPs, instant failover, traffic dials
+-- CloudFront: Edge caching; origin failover groups
+-- EC2 Auto Recovery: Same instance on new hardware
+-- ECS Circuit Breaker: Auto-rollback failed deployments
+-- Step Functions: Retry + Catch + compensating transactions
+-- Dead Letter Queues: Capture failed messages for retry
+-- Loose coupling: SQS, SNS, EventBridge between components
+-- Circuit breaker: Stop calling failing service; return fallback
+-- Bulkhead: Isolate failures (separate ASGs, accounts, regions)
+-- Retry with backoff: Exponential backoff + jitter
+-- Idempotency: Safe to retry without side effects
+-- Throttling: Rate limit to protect downstream services
+-- Graceful degradation: Serve partial/cached content on failure

column1: +-- AWS FIS: Controlled fault injection experiments
+-- Experiments: AZ failure, instance stop, network latency, CPU stress
+-- Stop conditions: CloudWatch alarms to halt experiment
+-- Process: Steady state -> Hypothesize -> Experiment -> Observe -> Improve
+-- Blast radius: Start small (single instance) -> expand (AZ -> region)
+-- GameDays: Team exercises simulating real failures
